title: gentiana germanica subsp. solstitialis
intro: Gentiana germanica subsp. solstitialis, also known as the summer gentian or Solstitial gentian, is a rare, perennial plant species belonging to the gentian family, characterized by its striking yellow flowers with a reddish-brown throat. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its digestive and respiratory properties, with its roots and stems being used to create medicinal preparations. The medicinal properties of Gentiana germanica subsp. solstitialis include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve nausea and vomiting, and act as an expectorant to help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are said to offer health benefits such as improving appetite, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall well-being.
